perturbation.original_text: Definition . "Confidential Information" includes, but is not limited to prototypes, trade secrets, intellectual property, information, technical data, research, products, software , services, development, macros, source code, unreleased software, inventions, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing , markets , customer information, business plans, business policies or practices, forecasts or financial information, team process, design process, part supply, pricing, development process and procedures , disclosed by Party One and Par t y T w o under this Agreement which at the time of disclosure is designated as confidential (or like designation), is disclosed in circumstances of confidence, or would be understood by the parties, exercising reasonable business judgment , to be confidentia l.
perturbation.changed_text: Definition . "Confidential Data" includes, but is not limited to prototypes, trade secrets, intellectual property, information, technical data, research, products, software , services, development, macros, source code, unreleased software, inventions, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing , markets , customer information, business plans, business policies or practices, forecasts or financial information, team process, design process, part supply, pricing, development process and procedures , disclosed by Party One and Par t y T w o under this Agreement which at the time of disclosure is designated as confidential (or like designation), is disclosed in circumstances of confidence, or would be understood by the parties, exercising reasonable business judgment , to be confidentia l.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates a contradiction because the agreement continues to use 'Confidential Information' throughout, particularly when defining obligations for protection. Section 3 uses the original term, creating uncertainty about whether 'Confidential Data' is included or excluded. This introduces ambiguity and potential for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder. Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication. Each party shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information of the other par ty where compelled by law to so disclose pursuant to any legal, judicial or administrative proceeding, provided the disclosing party provides all reasonable prior notice to the other party to allow it to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. Party One and P a r t y T w o pledges and agrees that it shall keep totally confidential any and all of the said information and other such matters arising hereto, and shall not disclose to any other person or persons, corporation, agent or consultant with whom it is or may be associated with, any such confidential information without express permission being given in writing by an authorized officer of other Party .
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information,' which is used in the contradicted text (Section 3) to establish obligations for protecting that information. The changed text redefines 'Confidential Information' as 'Confidential Data,' but Section 3 continues to use the original term 'Confidential Information.' This creates a clear contradiction because it is now unclear whether the obligations in Section 3 apply to 'Confidential Data,' leading to uncertainty about the scope of protection and potential disputes. The change directly alters a definition and breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder. Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication.
perturbation.changed_text: All releases of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity; (b) be disclosed only to employees of P a r t y T w o who have executed written confidentiality agreements wit h the employer; and (c) be reproduced or used by P a r t y T w o only to the extent necessary to fulfill it’s obligations hereunder. Party One and P a r t y Two shall protect shared Confidential Information with the same degree of care as it normally exercises to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but at a minimum with a reasonable degree of care to prevent its unauthorized use, dissemination or publication.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'disclosures' with 'releases,' the agreement now implies that all instances of sharing Confidential Information must be considered a 'release' rather than a standard 'disclosure'. While the rest of the clause implies a controlled disclosure, the 'release' term implies permission. This contradicts the intended confidentiality outlined in section 4, potentially opening a loophole where unauthorized sharing could be argued as an permitted 'release'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'disclosures' to 'releases' introduces a contradiction. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states that 'release' implies permission, conflicting with the intended confidentiality outlined in the 'Contradicted Text', which states that Party One and Party Two agree not to disclose confidential information without express written permission. This creates a loophole where unauthorized sharing could be argued as a permitted 'release', thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.changed_text: Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to utilize the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'use' with 'utilize' may imply a higher level of active use or implementation of the Confidential Information, while the original term might have included passive awareness or reference. This creates confusion. In Section 8, information is deemed not confidential if already known by the receiving party. If 'utilize' is interpreted narrowly, a party could argue that simply 'knowing' the information (even if they passively referenced it, a form of 'use') doesn't violate Section 4, contradicting the intent of protecting passively held confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information Not Confidential. Information shall be deemed not to be confidential if such information is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party, or is already known by the receiving party as evidenced by competent proof thereof, or is approved for rel ease by the prior written approval of the disclosing party, or is rightfully received by the receiving party from a third party without restriction and without breach of this agreement, or is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a sim ilar restriction on the rights of such third party, or is independently developed by the receiving party without the use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'use' to 'utilize' in Section 4 introduces a potential contradiction with Section 8. Section 8 states that information already known by the receiving party is not considered confidential. The explanation argues that 'utilize' can be interpreted as a more active engagement with the information, while 'use' could include passive awareness. If 'utilize' is interpreted narrowly, a party could argue that simply 'knowing' the information (a form of 'use') doesn't violate Section 4, even if they passively referenced it, contradicting the intent of protecting passively held confidential information as defined by Section 8, therefore the terms are misaligned creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All items, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'materials' to 'items' in Section 6 creates an inconsistency. While 'materials' clearly refers to tangible documents or physical items containing the confidential information, 'items' could be interpreted more broadly to include intangible things. However, the subsequent sentence specifically requests the return of 'all materials,' which implies the original, narrower definition. This contradiction makes it unclear whether the receiving party must return solely tangible materials or also confirm the deletion of intangible knowledge gained from the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Property Rights, Return of Materials . All items, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'materials' to 'items' creates a potential contradiction with the subsequent sentence that mandates the return of 'all materials.' While 'items' has a broader scope, the request to return 'materials' implies a narrower, more tangible definition. The Contradicted Text also includes this sentence. Therefore, the change in terminology introduces uncertainty regarding the return of intangible knowledge, creating an in-text contradiction because the changed text and contradicted text now conflict on what must be returned.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not approach to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'solicit' with 'approach' broadens the scope of prohibited actions. 'Solicit' generally implies actively seeking or requesting something, while 'approach' could mean any contact, even passive or accidental. However, the rest of the section still defines the term as 'solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with'. This in-text contradiction makes it unclear whether any contact with the other party's personnel, regardless of intent, is a violation, conflicting with the later clarification about generalized searches. It introduces confusion around the definition of the key term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-solicitation: Each party agrees that, for a period of 18 months following the date of this Agree ment, it shall not approach to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with any of the officers, employees, directors or consultants of the other party which whom such party has had contact or were identified to such party in connection with its evalu ation of a possible transaction between the parties. The term “solicit to employ or enter into a consulting arrangement with” shall not be deemed to include generalized searches by Party One for employees or consultants through media advertisements, emplo yment firms or otherwise, that are not focused on persons employed by or who consult for P a r t y T w o .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'solicit' to 'approach' significantly broadens the scope of the non-solicitation clause. The original text defines the term as 'solicit to employ,' which implies active seeking. The changed text uses 'approach to employ,' which is much broader and could include passive or accidental contact. The contradicted text is the same changed text. The definition following both statements continues to define the term as 'solicit to employ,' creating a contradiction. This discrepancy creates a significant in-text contradiction because the operative term, approach to employ, is now broader than its own definition, leading to ambiguity about what constitutes a violation.
